scope: global descriptions.json regen pulls in other sessions' folders
summary: generate-description is per-folder only by source. The global descriptions.json write is in folder-discovery via ensureDescriptionCache, which regenerates everything by scanning every base path. The fix is a scoped upsert plus a split between incremental update and full rebuild.

title: Add targeted global-cache upsert
problem: generate-description writes one per-folder description.json, but the global cache path regenerates everything: ensureDescriptionCache calls generateFolderDescriptions over every normalized base path and discovered folder.
evidence: generate-description.ts:37-39 and :108, ensureDescriptionCache folder-discovery.ts:1175-1179, generateFolderDescriptions walks all bases :674-740
proposal: Add upsertDescriptionCacheEntry(desc, folderPath, basePath) that loads descriptions.json, replaces or inserts only the target entry, sorts by specFolder, and writes only when that entry changed. Call it from generate-description after savePerFolderDescription.

title: Split incremental update from full rebuild
problem: isCacheStale treats any folder-set mismatch or newer mtime as global staleness, and tests assert full regeneration on stale, delete, and rename. So one-folder generation triggers a whole-tree rebuild.
evidence: isCacheStale folder-discovery.ts:1126-1143, discovered state :379-415, tests folder-discovery-integration.vitest.ts:380-438 and :496-515
proposal: Introduce updateDescriptionCacheEntry for known target writes and rebuildDescriptionCache for structural changes. Keep ensureDescriptionCache as the search-time fallback but stop using it as the write strategy for known one-folder generation.

title: Make cache writes deterministic and no-op safe
problem: generateFolderDescriptions captures now once and uses it for fallback lastUpdated and top-level generated, and generatePerFolderDescription stamps lastUpdated, so rebuilds change timestamps even when content did not change.
proposal: In the upsert compare the existing entry against the new one excluding volatile generated. If specFolder, description, keywords, and lastUpdated are unchanged, skip the write. Overlaps with a3-f2.

title: Add regression tests for scoped global index updates
problem: Existing tests cover full-cache create, regenerate, fresh-wins, and stale fallback, but none proves that generating one folder changes only one descriptions.json entry.
proposal: Add a test with three cached folders, update one through the new incremental path, and assert unrelated entries are byte-for-byte unchanged except allowed freshness metadata. Add fallback tests for missing or corrupt cache and deleted or renamed folders.
